title: Neoholmgrenia hilgardii
intro: Neoholmgrenia hilgardii, also known as Hill's gilia, is a perennial plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae) native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are said to promote wound healing, reduce pain and inflammation, and protect against cell damage. Bioactive constituents of Neoholmgrenia hilgardii include flavonoids, such as isorhapontigenin and kaempferol, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Neoholmgrenia hilgardii is often used in herbal preparations such as tinctures and infusions, as well as in topical creams and ointments, to support skin health and alleviate symptoms of skin conditions like eczema and acne.
